site.title: Ovellum
site.description: Markdown documentation for TypeScript and JavaScript projects. A merge engine that lets auto-generated API docs and hand-written prose coexist in the same files, plus a Jekyll-style static-site builder for purely manual docs.
site.footer: Ovellum is MIT-licensed. Built with itself.

hero.title: Documentation that doesn't drift.
hero.subtitle: Ovellum is an open-source documentation tool for TypeScript and JavaScript. Auto-generate from source, hand-write narrative pages, or mix both in the same file. Your prose never gets silently overwritten.

features.title: A Merge Engine
features.description: Tag sections of your Markdown as human-owned with a single comment pair. Ovellum updates the auto-generated parts around them; your prose survives every rebuild.

features.title: Three Modes
features.description: `auto` regenerates from source. `manual` builds a static site from Markdown. `hybrid` (default) merges the two. Switch per-project or per-file.

features.title: Orphans Quarantined
features.description: When you rename or delete a documented symbol, any hand-written prose tied to it gets archived to `.ovellum/orphans/`. Reviewable in PRs, recoverable any time.
